thinking.title: Assess thinking skills before learning programming
thinking.subtitle: This test does not assess Python syntax. It focuses on logic, patterns, conditions, algorithms, data reasoning, system thinking, visual reasoning, and debugging mindset.
thinking.question: Question {{index}}

thinkStudent001.question: A result in the homework tracker is wrong after several steps. What should you do first? Scenario code: STUDENT-001.

options.C: Check each step in order and find where the result first becomes wrong

thinkStudent001.explain: Debugging is systematic: reproduce, isolate, inspect evidence, and change one thing at a time.

thinkStudent002.question: You want to improve a school library. Which breakdown is the most useful? Scenario code: STUDENT-002.

options.B: Do everything at once without steps
options.D: Input needed information, apply rules, show result, check mistakes

thinkStudent002.explain: Good decomposition turns a broad problem into small, testable parts.

thinkStudent003.question: A change in the robot club affects data collection, validation, and reporting. Which action is best before release? Scenario code: STUDENT-003.

thinkStudent003.explain: System thinking looks at connected parts and the data or decisions flowing between them.

thinkStudent005.question: The science project has 48 items. You must apply the same rule to every item. What is the best algorithmic idea? Scenario code: STUDENT-005.

options.D: Change the rule after every item

thinkStudent005.explain: An algorithm should be repeatable, ordered, and easier to check than a random action.
